meta.title: Skills Manager · Desktop app for managing multi-Agent Skills
meta.description: Skills Manager — a desktop app for managing, distributing, and restoring skills across Claude, Cursor, Codex, OpenClaw, OpenCode, and other AI agents.
meta.ogTitle: Skills Manager · Your multi-Agent Skills HQ
meta.ogDescription: Manage local Skills in one place, distribute them across agents, and back them up with GitHub.

hero.rotatingAriaLabel: Skills Manager, Hub, Share, Switch, Sync, Backup, Repo
hero.sub:
Scan local Skills in one place, distribute them across agents, and back them up with GitHub.
Turn scattered skill folders into a reusable, portable library.
hero.previewHint: This sandbox preview shows the real UI and won't read or touch your local Skills.

features.title: One window to manage Skills across every Agent
features.lead: Bring Skills scattered across different agent folders into one place for management, distribution, and backup.

cards.title: One-click share to any Agent
cards.body: Master / sub switches cover both bulk and fine-grained control. Link any Skill to Cursor, Codex or Claude in a single click.

cards.title: GitHub backup & cross-machine sync
cards.body: Push local Skills to GitHub in one click, then pull them back on a new machine. The same repo can serve as both your personal backup and a shared team skill library.

cards.title: Drag-and-drop import · zero config
cards.body: Drop any folder containing a SKILL.md onto the window and it's imported in seconds — no CLI, no path arguments.

cards.title: Flat & by-source dual views
cards.body: Flat view answers "what Skills do I have"; by-source view answers "what's inside each Agent". Switch perspective without losing context.

views1.title:
Scan every local Agent
and aggregate Skills into one list
views1.lead: Aggregated by Skill, not by source. See what's already collected and what's still scattered across agents at a glance.

bullets.html: Search & filter instant keyword search, filter by enabled state
See every Agent at a glance each card shows the enabled state across all currently supported agents
Tri-state master switch gray / yellow / red clearly mark disabled / native-only / partially-or-fully enabled

views2.title:
Curious what's really
inside each Agent? One click away
views2.lead: Grouped by source so you can instantly see where a Skill lives and decide what belongs in the central repo versus what should stay local.

bullets.html: Copy to central repo into a unified library that every Agent can reuse
Native Skill indicator a yellow dot signals "this one ships with the Agent"
Action log every "copy to central" action is recorded and traceable

detail.title:
Skill detail · file tree +
per-Agent state
detail.lead: Click any Skill to view its full file tree, SKILL.md contents, and how it's shared across Agents. The side panel is drag-resizable to fit any screen.

bullets.html: Inline file preview click a file to read its content, no external editor needed
Delete protection Agent folders are read-only by default; flip Advanced Mode to enable precise deletes

github.title:
Turn your Skills
into a shareable repository
github.lead: Connect your GitHub repo for one-click sync · one-click restore. The same repo can act as both your personal backup and a shared source for a team.

bullets.html: Sync to GitHub push every central Skill to the remote with incremental commits
Restore from GitHub pull all your Skills back on a fresh machine
Shared distribution keep personal and team Skills aligned around one repository

dragdrop.title: Drop it in. Done.
dragdrop.lead: No CLI required. Drag any folder containing a SKILL.md from your file manager onto the window and it's imported and ready to share with other Agents.

bullets.html: Instant feedback the list silently refreshes after import, no loading flicker
Batch drop drop multiple folders at once; failures are reported individually

settings.title:
Memory filters · delete protection ·
Advanced Mode all included
settings.lead: Skills Manager joins your agent setup in read-only mode by default. Turn on Advanced Mode only when you need to write or delete.

bullets.html: Memory filters hide temporary Skills injected by CLIs or workflows
中/EN toggle · dark mode follow the system or switch manually
Zero setup no account required, configure and go

ctaBanner.title: Take charge of your Skills today
ctaBanner.body: Free, open-source, cross-platform. Grab the latest installer from GitHub Releases or clone and build it yourself.

items.a: No — it's fully free and open-source under MIT License, with the repo public on GitHub. Use, modify and redistribute as you like. Issues and PRs are welcome.

items.q: Will it modify my existing local Skills?
items.a: By default, no. The app scans Agent folders in observer mode and only creates symlinks or copies when you explicitly opt in (flip a switch, drag to import, copy to central). Delete protection is on by default; writes require flipping Advanced Mode under Settings.
Claude, Cursor, Codex, OpenClaw, and OpenCode are supported today. Any Agent whose Skill folder follows the SKILL.md convention should work in principle — PRs for new Agents are welcome.

items.q: Does GitHub backup require a paid account?
items.a: No. Any personal GitHub account works, whether the repo is public or private. If you want to use the repo as a shared source for others, a public repo is usually more convenient.
Installers ship for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) and Windows 10+. Linux users can clone the repo and run npm run tauri:build to build locally.

items.q: How is it different from a CLI script or another Skill manager?
items.a: The key difference is "cross-Agent perspective + desktop-grade interaction". Most CLIs handle one Agent at a time; Skills Manager brings every Agent's Skills into a single UI, with tri-state master switches, per-Agent toggles and drag-and-drop — no more folder hunting or command typing.
